channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: astrocp: Conformal Prediction con cobertura condicional por clase para astronomía
item.description: astrocp: Conformal Prediction con cobertura condicional por clase para astronomía Una librería pequeña (AD-MCP) que da conjuntos de predicción con cobertura por cada clase en datasets astronómicos...

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Squid Washing Up on Cape Cod Beach
item.description: Lots of articles about this. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered. Blog moderation policy.

item.title: Video Commerce with Medusa v2 & MeiliSearch: Smart Product Matching
item.description: Advanced Medusa v2 Architecture Transform video content into direct sales. Engineer an intelligent, fully-automated video sync pipeline using MeiliSearch's semantic matching to instantly link...

item.title: A Two-Stage Workflow to Detect Data Leakage.
item.description: Data leakage is the silent killer of machine learning models. You finish training, see an incredible 98% accuracy on your test set, and feel like a genius, until the model hits production and...

item.title: Colibri: Running a 744B AI Model on Your Laptop
item.description: GLM-5.2 is a frontier AI model with 744 billion parameters. It normally requires H100 GPUs, hundreds of gigabytes of VRAM, and a cloud bill larger than a car payment. Colibri runs it on a laptop...

item.title: Neden LIWICODE'u Geliştirdim? Hafif Kod Analiz Aracı ve Dev Visual Studio Karşılaştırması
item.description: Selamlar yazılımcı dostlar! 👋 Geliştiriciler olarak araçlarımızı çok seviyoruz. Ancak sadece belirli bir kod bloğunu analiz etmek, mantıksal yapısını kontrol etmek veya performansını hızlıca...

item.title: BMad Method vs AI-DLC: Two AI Development Frameworks Compared
item.description: AI coding agents are everywhere. ChatGPT, Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Amazon Q. They write code, debug, and review. But they do not scale. A bug fix is not an enterprise system, yet most...

item.title: Why I Built LIWICODE: A Lightweight Code Analysis Tool & How It Holds Up Against Heavyweight Visual Studio
item.description: Hey DEV community! 👋 As developers, we love our tools, but we also know the pain of waiting for massive IDEs to spin up just to analyze or benchmark a specific block of code. A while back, I set...

item.title: The Bus Factor: How to Actually Find It and Fix It on Your Team
item.description: Your bus factor is not theoretical. It is a business continuity metric, and unlike most culture-y engineering concepts, it's one you can actually pin down with a bit of investigation. Bus factor =...

item.title: Are You Missing Out on Agent Skills? Here's How They Work
item.description: Hello, I'm Rijul. I'm building git-lrc, a micro AI code reviewer that runs on every commit. It's free and source-available on GitHub. Star git-lrc to help more developers discover the project. Do...

item.title: How I Survived the Silent AI Upgrades (And Shifted My Workflow)
item.description: The Day the Premium Quotas Melted We’ve all seen the massive marketing headlines celebrating multi-million token context windows. So when I scored access to the Google AI Pro student tier, I...

item.title: OpenSSL HollowByte Flaw Could Freeze Server Memory with 11-Byte TLS Requests
item.description: Eleven bytes will make an unpatched OpenSSL server set aside up to 131 KB of memory for a message that never arrives. On the glibc systems Okta tested, that memory is gone until the process...

item.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
item.description: Agility is opening a new training center for its Digit robots in Fremont, California.

item.title: Shark’s versatile ChillPill cooling system is back to its best price
item.description: Portable fans are one of the easiest ways to stay cool during the summer, and you don’t have to spend much to find a decent one. If you’re looking for something more versatile, though, Shark’s...

item.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
item.description: India's smartphone slowdown highlights how the AI boom is reshaping consumer electronics, from pricing and demand to corporate strategy.

item.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
item.description: TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted by social media consultant Matt Navarra. The tool is initially being...

item.title: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says his 30-day warranty is all about trust
item.description: Pebble founder Eric Migicovsky says buyers of its new e-paper smartwatches should know what they're signing up for and trust Pebble to make things right if they run into issues, despite the short...

item.title: Seven Malicious Vite npm Packages Use Blockchain C2 to Deliver a RAT
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a cluster of seven malicious npm packages targeting the Vite frontend tooling ecosystem as part of a software supply chain attack. The malicious package...

item.title: Apple Music is getting a price hike
item.description: Apple Music is more expensive now. In the US, an individual plan now costs $11.99 per month, a $1 bump up from the previous $10.99 price. A family plan now costs $19.99 per month, up from $16.99,...

item.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
item.description: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI last Friday, and it’s not messing around. The complaint alleges a pattern of misconduct reaching all the way up to OpenAI’s chief hardware...

item.title: Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
item.description: Apple is suing OpenAI. The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done. So what does...

item.title: New NadMesh Botnet Hunts Exposed AI Services for Cloud Keys and Kubernetes Tokens
item.description: A Go botnet called NadMesh turned up in early July hunting exposed AI services, and the operator's own dashboard claims 3,811 unique AWS keys. A Shodan harvester keeps the scan queue stocked with...

item.title: Asus’ top-end 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor is $400 off
item.description: The Asus ROG Swift 32-inch 4K QD-OLED gaming monitor has almost everything I want in a high-end gaming monitor, most notably a QD-OLED panel for inky black levels and vivid colors that go beyond...

item.title: GoldenEyeDog Subgroup Linked to DigiCert Breach and Code-Signing Certificate Theft
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have attributed the April 2026 DigiCert security incident to a threat activity cluster dubbed CylindricalCanine. Expel, which shared technical details of the event,...

item.title: Fine-tune video and image models at scale with NVIDIA NeMo Automodel and 🤗 Diffusers
Samsung’s redesigned Z Fold 8 with a wide display just leaked
item.description: Leaker Evan Blass shared images of Samsung's redesigned Galaxy Z Fold 8 just days before the July 22nd launch event where Samsung is expected to officially announce the phone. The leaked images...

item.title: Is America ready for this quirky Jeep-looking EV that can park itself?
item.description: Are we living through a small car renaissance? There's the Slate Truck, Amble's dune buggy, and the Fiat Topolino, as well as a whole galaxy of kei cars and trucks from Japan that have their own...

item.title: Florida man arrested for allegedly stealing over $200,000 in crypto using Steam game malware
item.description: Federal authorities have arrested a Florida man suspected of stealing at least $220,000 in crypto through malware-infected Steam games, as reported earlier by local news outlet Local10. In the...

item.title: Patreon stops asking AI bots not to scrape — and starts blocking them
item.description: Patreon is strengthening its defenses against AI scraping by working with Cloudflare to block bots that train AI models on creators’ content without permission. The move marks a shift away from...

item.title: Over half a million power tool batteries have been recalled due to a USB-C charging fire risk
item.description: Greenworks Tools has issued a recall for around 554,780 Kobalt-branded power tools designed for yard work because of a battery issue "posing a risk of serious injury from fire hazard." There have...
